description: Strings or structs are passed as raw integers and decoded with the wrong length or encoding, reading adjacent memory.
body: Failure mode: code hand-passes a value across the boundary as a single integer or a pointer with a mismatched length/encoding, so the receiver reads the wrong number of bytes or interprets UTF-8 as something else, pulling in adjacent linear-memory bytes as data and silently corrupting the result. Detection signals: garbled strings or wrong struct fields after a boundary call; off-by-N byte errors; manual pointer math instead of generated bindings. Do not encode complex values as a bare integer. Safer fallback: use generated bindings (wasm-bindgen) or pass explicit pointer+length with a fixed encoding and an ownership rule, and verify with a round-trip test that the value comes back identical.
